# Phase 7 Task 0: Create Core Calendar Activities Actions Vue Component

## Task Overview

Create the main `components/calendar-activities-actions.vue` Vue Single File Component (SFC) that serves as the primary UI interface for displaying and filtering CBD activities and actions data. This component will be the foundational element for the calendar system, implementing proper Vue 3 Composition API with TypeScript.

## Context

This task implements the core UI component defined in Phase 7 of the CBD Calendar of Activities & Actions project roadmap. The component serves as a reusable, standalone interface that can be consumed both within the Nuxt.js application and as a library component for external use. It must handle the merged dataset from previous phases (activities, actions, and meeting data) and provide an intuitive interface for users to view and filter the information.

The component needs to integrate with the query service from Phase 6 and support the data structures established in earlier phases. It should follow Nuxt.js and Vue.js best practices, implement proper TypeScript typing, and be designed for both Secretariat users (internal) and Party users (public viewing).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Component file created at `components/calendar-activities-actions.vue` following Vue 3 SFC structure
- Implements `<script setup>` with TypeScript (`lang="ts"`)
- Properly typed props interface for data input and configuration options
- Component loads and renders without errors in both development and production environments
- Supports both client-side and server-side rendering (SSR) compatibility
- Implements proper Vue 3 Composition API patterns with reactive data handling
- Component emits appropriate events for parent component communication
- Props validation and default values properly defined
- Component is accessible via auto-import in Nuxt.js environment
- Basic component structure includes template, script, and style sections
- Implements proper component naming conventions (PascalCase)
- Component can accept external data sources or fetch data internally

## Implementation Outline

1. **Component Structure Setup**
   - Create the Vue SFC file with proper template, script setup, and style sections
   - Configure TypeScript with `lang="ts"` attribute
   - Set up proper component naming and export

2. **Props and Types Definition**
   - Define TypeScript interfaces for component props
   - Create interfaces for data structures (activities, actions, merged records)
   - Implement props validation using `defineProps<T>()` syntax
   - Set up default values for optional props

3. **Data Management Setup**
   - Initialize reactive data using Vue 3 Composition API (`ref`, `reactive`, `computed`)
   - Set up data loading mechanism (props vs internal fetch)
   - Implement data transformation logic for display format
   - Create computed properties for derived data

4. **Basic Template Structure**
   - Create semantic HTML structure for the calendar interface
   - Implement proper accessibility markup (ARIA labels, roles)
   - Set up container structure for filters and data display
   - Add loading and error states

5. **Event Emission Setup**
   - Define component events using `defineEmits<T>()`
   - Implement event handlers for user interactions
   - Set up proper event payload typing

6. **Initial Styling Framework**
   - Include Bootstrap 5 utility classes for base styling
   - Set up scoped styles section
   - Implement CSS custom properties for theming support
   - Create responsive design foundation

7. **Integration Points**
   - Set up integration with Phase 6 query service
   - Implement data fetching from public data endpoints
   - Create interfaces for external library usage

## Test Strategy

- **Unit Tests**: Component mounting, props validation, computed properties, event emission
- **Integration Tests**: Data loading, external service integration, event handling
- **Component Tests**: Template rendering, conditional display logic, error states
- **Accessibility Tests**: Screen reader compatibility, keyboard navigation, ARIA attributes
- **Performance Tests**: Component initialization time, memory usage with large datasets

## Technical Requirements

- Vue 3.4+ with Composition API
- TypeScript 5.0+ with strict type checking
- Bootstrap 5 for styling framework
- Nuxt.js 3.8+ compatibility
- ESLint compliance with Nuxt configuration
- Component must be tree-shakeable for library builds

## Files to Create/Modify

- `components/calendar-activities-actions.vue` (new)
- `shared/types/component-props.ts` (new, if not exists)
- Update component auto-imports in `nuxt.config.ts` if needed

## Dependencies

- Vue 3 Composition API
- TypeScript
- Bootstrap 5 CSS framework
- Nuxt.js component auto-import system

## Risks and Mitigation

- **Risk**: Component too complex for single implementation
  - **Mitigation**: Start with minimal viable component, iterate with additional features
- **Risk**: TypeScript complexity with Vue 3 setup
  - **Mitigation**: Use proven patterns from Vue 3 documentation, implement incremental typing
- **Risk**: SSR/hydration issues
  - **Mitigation**: Follow Nuxt.js SSR best practices, test both client and server rendering

## References

- [Vue 3 Composition API Documentation](https://vuejs.org/guide/introduction.html)
- [Nuxt.js Components Documentation](https://nuxt.com/docs/guide/directory-structure/components)

## Additional Information Needed

To make this task more accurate and complete, the following additional information would be helpful:

1. **Data Schema Details**
   - Exact structure of merged data records from Phase 6
   - Required vs optional fields in the data model
   - Expected data volume (number of records)

2. **Design Specifications**
   - UI/UX wireframes or mockups for the component
   - Color scheme and branding guidelines
   - Specific Bootstrap theme or customizations

3. **Performance Requirements**
   - Expected load time benchmarks
   - Maximum number of records to display simultaneously
   - Pagination vs infinite scroll preferences

4. **Integration Requirements**
   - Specific events that parent components need to listen for
   - External API endpoints for data fetching
   - Authentication requirements for data access

5. **Browser Support**
   - Minimum browser versions to support
   - Polyfill requirements for older browsers

6. **Accessibility Standards**
   - Specific WCAG compliance level required (A, AA, AAA)
   - Screen reader testing requirements
   - Keyboard navigation specifications

7. **Internationalization**
   - Multi-language support requirements
   - Date/time format localization needs
   - Text direction support (LTR/RTL)

8. **Error Handling**
   - Specific error scenarios to handle
   - User feedback mechanisms for errors
   - Fallback content for failed data loads
